draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer clean, low-contrast UI for focused work sessions
facade.label: Minimalist dark sidebar
facade.acceptImplies: Explore darker, reduced-visual-weight interfaces with strong focus areas
facade.rejectImplies: Test brighter, more visually generous layouts with distributed information density

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer clean, low-contrast UI for focused work sessions
evidence.implication: Explore darker, reduced-visual-weight interfaces with strong focus areas

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a CRM that feels like a trusted peer, not enterprise software—using conversational language and personality over corporate formality
facade.label: Casual, warm tone
facade.acceptImplies: Design voice with contractions, humor, and approachability; use 'you' and direct address; avoid jargon and corporate speak
facade.rejectImplies: Design voice should be polished, formal, and authoritative; prioritize clarity and professionalism over personality; minimize casual language and humor

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a persistent navigation sidebar for client/project switching, with tabbed content areas inside that main panel rather than full-page tabs
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure (sidebar vs tabs) combined with information hierarchy depth
facade.label: Sidebar with tabs
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick project context-switching and consistent global navigation; probe deeper into sidebar width, collapsibility, and tab behavior within it
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer top-level tabs, full-width layouts, or different navigation entirely; explore horizontal tab systems or card-based navigation next

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need to see project deadlines and milestones as a visual calendar, not just in a data table
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data vs. key metrics — whether to show all project dates in a scannable calendar format or summarize key deadlines in a simple list
facade.label: Client timeline view
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should prioritize visual timeline/calendar representations for project planning and deadline tracking
facade.rejectImplies: The CRM should rely on simple lists, tables, or text-based deadline summaries without calendar visualization

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer fluid page transitions over instant loads because it creates a sense of continuity and reduces cognitive jarring when managing multiple client contexts
facade.label: Smooth slide-ins
facade.acceptImplies: Design heavy use of entrance/exit animations, staggered reveals, and motion-based hierarchy throughout the CRM interface
facade.rejectImplies: Prioritize snappy, zero-latency interactions with instant state changes and minimal animation, favoring speed and directness over motion

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond to interfaces that feel personally supportive rather than corporate, with soft lighting and approachable typography that builds trust during repetitive CRM tasks
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: The design should lean into human-centered warmth: cream backgrounds, soft shadows, rounded corners, welcoming microcopy. Trust and comfort are key to adoption.
facade.rejectImplies: Cool, clinical aesthetics (high contrast, sharp edges, corporate blue) may feel alienating or overly formal for independent workers seeking a tool that respects their autonomy.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers working long hours need visual warmth to reduce screen fatigue, even within dark interfaces
facade.axisTargeted: Color temperature (cool vs warm) within dark UI
facade.label: Warm card backgrounds
facade.acceptImplies: Explore cream/beige card surfaces against dark sidebars; test warm accent colors for CTAs and highlights
facade.rejectImplies: Stick with pure black/neutral grays; prioritize maximum contrast over visual comfort

axes.label: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels more inviting and branded
Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure—reveals if they want everything visible at once or prefer guided, step-by-step navigation
Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/supportive tone—reveals if they want business-like efficiency or human encouragement
Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals if they want zen focus or inspiring momentum
Whether they want financial/metrics-first design vs. client-relationship-first design—reveals what data matters most to their mental model
Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered animations—reveals if they want speed or polish

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo
